entry.title: Understanding HANA memory related license utilization in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
entry.content:
When using SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, your license usage depends on several factors. These include counts of different user types, business objects, service calls, or how much you use system resources. In this blog, we'll focus on the last factor: resource usage.

Specifically, we'll look at how memory-related license metrics are calculated and answer some common questions about it.

The way memory usage is measured in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition has evolved, and there are now three different methods co-existing: C414 for 2-System Landscape (2SL), and CY263 or CY500 for 3-System Landscape (3SL). The method that applies to you depends on your contract. This blog will explain all three methods, starting with general information that applies to all of them, and then diving into the specifics of each.

Fig. 1: Example of memory utilization metric in license dashboard

It is not easy to get transparency on the reasons for growing memory license consumption. Therefore we will discuss the available options to identify them in a separate blog. And in another blog, we will go over the existing tools for reducing data .

Introduction

License consumption must-knows

No matter which license is relevant to your system, the following must be known before reading the part relevant to your SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition system:

  • License utilization details are available in SAP for Me. See further information here: License Utilization Information.
  • The “entitlement” also known as “subscription” is the contractual maximum of the license. We use the term “entitlement” in this blog.
  • The “consumption” also referred to as “measurement” is the current usage of the entitlement.
  • The memory entitlement is checked against the production system only. The sizes of other systems in your landscape such as Test, Dev or Quality systems are not considered, i.e. the size of the non-productive systems is not added up. In extreme cases, the memory consumption of the test system may be larger than the production one (e.g. shortly before go-live) and this situation will not affect the memory related license metric.
  • If the consumption is above the entitlement, e.g. due to growing data volumes or intensive usage of the system, SAP will continue to provide the required memory to prevent performance problems. At this point, either a reduction of consumption must be made, or an extension of the entitlement must be acquired.
  • All memory-related license metrics are based on SAP HANA database memory. Memory requirements or usage for other parts of your SAP S/4HANA landscape, such as the ABAP Application Servers, are not included in these metrics.
  • The unit used in license measurement is a Gigabyte (GB). 1 GB = 1.000.000.000 bytes.

Main components of the HANA database from a license perspective

In this blog, the following picture will be used to explain the differences between the various consumption metrics.  

Fig 2.: Simplified view of SAP HANA components relevant for license consumption

This graphic illustrates disk consumption in the bottom part (grey) and memory consumption in the upper part (blue and green).

Note: all data of an SAP HANA database is always stored on disk. When starting the SAP HANA database the data from disk is loaded fully or partially to the HANA Memory to be available for fast processing.

The memory (top) part has the following components:

  • Used Memory - Data Footprint: (dark blue color) Data loaded from HANA disk to HANA memory.
  • Used Memory - Processing Memory: (light blue color) HANA memory is not only used to have the data available for fast access but is also required for management of the HANA DB and for processing queries, e.g. storing intermediate results or aggregations. This component can be as large as the sum of the data components (aka data footprint) in memory.
  • Allocated Memory: (light green) This is memory that is still free but already reserved (i.e. allocated) for this database to allow future growth or peak load during upgrades. It is usually between 30 and 50% of the total capacity provided.
  • Available Memory: This is memory that could be added to the database dynamically if required.

The data footprint consists of the following 3 types of data:

  • Cold Data: In this context, LOB data: Large Objects. Typically, ABAP coding or large interface messages such as xml, JSON documents or job spool requests, ... It is usually read once and hardly ever again. It has a disk to memory ratio larger than 20 to 1.
  • Warm Data: In this context, NSE data: Native Storage Extension. Typically, this is all sort of log data, such as change documents, application log, security audit log… This data is rarely read. It has a disk to memory ratio of 5 to 1.
  • Hot Data: In this context, this is Column and Row Store Data. Mostly business data that is read, used, changed and created by the users when using apps, by background jobs, and by API calls. It has a disk to memory ratio close to 1 to 1.

Note that there is no aging mechanism to move data from cold to warm or hot or reverse. Data is created as hot, warm or cold by definition. This distinction is made only in this blog for explanatory purposes as the 'data temperature' can play a role whether data is included in the license metric or not.

And there is an additional dimension to the data components described above:

  • SAP data: Essential part of the system that is completely independent of any business activity. For example, the UI5 and ABAP code, apps metadata, in-app help texts and their translations, object definitions, system logs... The size and the content of this data cannot be influenced by SAP customers. SAP decides which data is stored and for how long.
  • Customer data - also known as Customer Business Data: Data populated by SAP customers’ business processes. The data is ‘owned’ by SAP’s customers, and it is their responsibility to decide how this data should be handled, e.g. if and when this data should be archived or deleted (i.e. retention management).

How does SAP distinguish between SAP data and Customer data?

Historically, one SAP S/4HANA installation for On-Premises customers could be used by different companies as the data model includes a field to allow data segregation. The so-called ‘client’ or ‘mandant’ field is part of most tables. The ‘customer owned data’ is the sum of the data in the customer client (100), i.e. your productive working client.

Some tables might have both customer and SAP data (For example, data in client 000 and client 100). In such case, the license measurement (for C414 and CY500) counts the entries and proportionally calculates the size of both. Tables without a client field are SAP owned.

FAQ: What about attachments and outputs?

Outputs or GOS attachments and DMS documents, such as PDF or MS-Word or MS-Excel, are not stored in the SAP HANA database and therefore are not considered in the licenses discussed in this blog. Instead, they are stored in the BTP Document Management Service (DMS) via an SAP managed communication scenario. Alternatively, it is possible to connect a CMIS certified repository of choice, e.g. for sharing a companywide repository across different solutions via SAP_COM_0597.

Note, to increase the speed of creating output documents, e.g. printouts of billing documents, these are temporarily stored as LOB data (Cold Data on the picture) within the SAP HANA database and are offloaded to the external storage repository several times a day, so they don’t have a relevant impact on memory usage.

2-System Landscape - (Metric ID C414)

The metric you can see in your licensing dashboard for a 2-system landscape (2SL) system (C414) represents the disk size of customer owned Warm and Hot Data.

The licensing metric displayed in SAP for Me is kept stable by excluding the two highest consumption peak values of the past 30 days.

Measurement scope

Fig 3. 2-system landscape - components included in license metric C414

C414 advantages:

  • The “SAP Data” is not part of the consumption measurement.
  • Cold Data is not part of the consumption measurement.

C414 disadvantages:

  • The complete size of the Warm Data is part of the consumption measurement. No matter if only a small portion of it is loaded to memory.

The minimum entitlement of C414 is 100 GB and can be larger if system extension or more FUEs are purchased.

FAQ: Why is there a sudden increase in the C414 license measure in August 2024?

Due to changes in upgrade techniques, over time, more and more data became no longer visible to the C414 license measurement. It was misclassified as SAP Data. With 2408, a correction was implemented that made this data visible again. This means the August peak you might see in your license measurement is not reflecting a sudden data growth but instead made visible a data growth that existed for many months or years.

3-System Landscape – (Metric ID CY263)

The metric you can see in your licensing dashboard for 3-system landscape (3SL) systems (CY263) represents the total HANA memory provided to your tenant to ensure that the tenant can be operated without memory shortages. Technically, this is the “indexserver Process Allocation Limit” or PAL.

The licensing metric displayed in SAP for Me is kept stable by excluding the two highest consumption peak values of the past 30 days.

Measurement scope

The measurement includes:

  • Cold, Warm and Hot Data memory footprint
  • Processing memory
  • Free memory

The reason for changing the approach from ‘data footprint-based’ (2-system landscape C414) to ‘full-memory-usage’ (3-system landscape CY263) was the increased options for system extensibility. With SAP S/4HANA Cloud ABAP Environment, it is possible for SAP customers to define their own custom objects. Execution of complex queries on custom CDS views may lead to a significant and unpredictable consumption of the processing memory of the SAP HANA database. To reflect this in the license consumption, this part of the HANA memory was added to the metric measurement.

Fig. 4: 3-system landscape - components included in license metric CY263

CY263 advantages:

  • The license consumption mirrors precisely the system’s cost as everything needed to operate the system is included in the measurement.
  • Only the part loaded to memory of the Cold and Warm Data is measured.

CY263 disadvantages:

  • The measurement does not start at 0. The system is provisioned with a minimum of 150 GB.
  • SAP Data and Customer Business data are not distinguished.
  • The measurement depends on SAP’s decision of how much free memory shall be allocated to the system. SAP operates the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition tenants in a way that the memory requirements are monitored regularly and adjusted automatically. It cannot be influenced by you as SAP customer.
  • HANA memory intensive queries on CDS views may increase the memory consumption, although the data footprint has not changed. This can be complicated to identify.

The minimum entitlement of CY263 is 200 GB and is extended if system extension or more FUEs are purchased.

FAQ: How does the CY263 measurement relate to the figures displayed in Cloud Application Lifecycle Management (Cloud ALM) Health Monitoring?

The metric ‘HANA Memory Used’ in Health Monitoring of Cloud ALM provides the current HANA memory usage (99.78 GB in this example) compared to the CY263 entitlement (200 GB in this example). The CY263 consumption is not displayed in this dashboard.

Fig. 5: Cloud Application Lifecycle Management (CALM) view on used memory

The Memory used can be compared to the sum of the blue boxes in Figure 4. (All 3 types of Data in memory and the Processing Memory). It does not include the free and available memory. And it shows the current memory and not the recent peak

3-System Landscape Starting June 2025 – (Metric ID CY500)

The metric you can see in your licensing dashboard for 3SL systems (CY500) for contracts signed after June 2025 represents the current data memory footprint of customer owned data.

The value displayed in SAP for Me is the maximum value measured every day.

Measurement scope

The measurement includes:

  • The memory usage of Customer Hot Data
  • The memory usage of Customer Warm Data
  • 5% of the disk size of Customer Cold Data

Fig. 6: 3-system landscape - components included in license metric CY500

CY500 advantages:

  • The consumption measurement only considers customer data and is more relatable to the system usage.
  • Only a fraction of the Cold Data is part of the consumption measurement. This fraction is included to take into account rare cases where Cold Data becomes extremely large.

CY500 disadvantages:

  • Processing memory is not part of the license but is nonetheless physically limited.

The minimum entitlement of CY500 is 16 GB (for 25 users) and is extended based on the number of users.

FAQ: Why is the minimum entitlement for CY500 much smaller than the one for CY263?

Around 150 GB of memory must be provided to an empty S/4HANA Public Cloud system to operate without memory shortages. This requirement is caused by the high memory usage of Near-Zero Downtime upgrades and Hotfix implementations during which a large part of the system is duplicated. CY500 does not include SAP Data and starts at zero.

With a minimum entitlement of 200 GB for CY263, the actual memory available for growth is around 50 GB. An increase of 1 GB of customer business data might mean an increase of another 1 GB of required processing memory (As described by Sizing 1793345). Therefore, the space available for customer business data is about 25 GB. And this is comparable to the minimum value of CY500.

FAQ: We archived a lot of data, why is the reduction on the license measurement smaller than expected?

There are various reasons for this:

  • CY500 and CY263 are both used to measure memory usage. If the data archived is of type Cold or Warm Data, there is a high chance that this data was not in memory but on disk. Therefore, memory usage might have been reduced much less than disk usage.

If you use the ILM Advisor app, be aware that it shows currently the object size as calculated for C414. Metric C414 measures disk usage, so archiving should have a direct impact on this metric and the values displayed in ILM Advisor app.

  • The values displayed in SAP for Me for Metrics C414 and CY263 are the maximum values over the last 30 days. Therefore, it takes 30 days to see a decrease. In between, other data might have grown and that will make the decrease visually smaller.
  • The sizes displayed in “Manage Archiving variant” show the size of the archive file. It does not have a 1-to-1 relation to the reduction of memory usage in HANA as different compression algorithms are used.
  • Depending on the archiving object, it can also be that the deletion in the SAP S/4HANA system was not done yet and will happen with the archiving of other related objects. For example archiving of controlling related archiving objects, e.g. COPA2_A000 for profitability analysis or CO_TRANS for cost-center line items do not delete records but do updates to allow a later final archiving of accounting documents (archiving object FI_DOCUMNT)

Conclusion

We are aware that it unfortunately requires quite a bit of in-depth understanding of the SAP HANA database architecture to be able to put the activities performed on your SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition system e.g. data archiving, posting business documents, creating logs, performing database queries in relation to the effect on the licensing. Still, we hope that this blog could provide first insights and helps with a better understanding.

We will discuss the available options to identify memory consumers in a separate blog. And in another blog, we will go over the existing tools for reducing data and will provide hands-on guidance on how to reduce commonly growing data e.g. Security Audit Logs or Application Integration Framework (AIF) data.

entry.title: Beginner’s Guide to SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud Test Automation for Functional Consultants and Testers
entry.content:
Introduction

In the world of SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud, change is constant—monthly delivery of innovations, localisation updates, and fast-paced deployments. Ensuring business continuity and quality assurance in such a dynamic environment is no longer optional—it's essential.

SAP’s Test Automation Tool (TAT), embedded within the S/4HANA Public Cloud system, empowers functional consultants and testers to build automated test cases without coding and execute them as part of release cycles or ongoing change initiatives.

In this post, I’ll walk you through what TAT is, why it matters, and how you can start automating your first business process—with no need to install anything externally.

What is the Test Automation Tool for SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud ?

SAP's built-in Test Automation Tool (TAT) is a cloud-native feature available for all S/4HANA Public Cloud customers. It's designed to automate business process testing without any third-party tools.

Key features include:

  • Pre-delivered test scripts for standard business processes (like Procure-to-Pay, Order-to-Cash)
  • A graphical UI to record and modify test steps
  • Support for reusable test components and variants
  • Integration with SAP Cloud ALM for test orchestration
  • Test analytics for tracking pass/fail results

Why Use TAT ?

Benefit

Description

Speed

Automate in hours what used to take days manually

Resilience

Standard test scripts get updated with SAP releases

Compliance

Easy to run tests before and after upgrades

Low-Code

Functional users can build and update tests without ABAP skills

Step-by-Step: How to Create Your First Test Script

Let’s say you want to automate a scenario - Procurement of Direct Materials in the system

🔹Step 1: Open the Test Automation Tool App

  • Launch the SAP Fiori Launchpad.
  • Open the Automated Testing space.
    • Pre-requisite: Ensure your user is assigned the role SAP_BR_ADMIN_TEST_AUTOMATION to access the test automation features.
  • Navigate to the “Manage Your Test Processes” app to start working with test scripts.

🔹Step 2: Choose a Pre-Delivered Test Script

  • Use the search function to locate a relevant SAP-delivered test script, such as:
    • “Procurement of Direct Materials” or “J45”
  • Review the process steps to ensure it matches your requirements.

🔹Step 3: Copy and Customise the Test Script

  • Click “Copy” to create your own Test Process as a custom version.
  • Modify the test process:
    • Adjust steps to suit your business scenario
    • Enter required input parameters (e.g., supplier, material number, plant, quantity)
  • Make the Test Script Visible.

🔹Step 4: Create a Test Plan

  • Open the “Test Your Processes” app.
  • Click the “+” (Create) button to define a new Test Plan.
  • Search and select your customised test script from Step 3.
  • Click “Save” to save your Test Plan.

🔹Step 5: Execute the Test

  • Open your saved Test Plan.
  • Click “Execute”.

🔹Step 6: Review Test Results

  • Go to the “Test Results” - > “Logs” section.
  • Review:
    • Pass/Fail status for each step
    • Execution logs for troubleshooting
    • Screenshots captured during test run for audit and validation

Best Practices for Success

✅ Use Pre-Delivered Scripts
Don't reinvent the wheel. Start from SAP-delivered templates and tailor them.

✅ Maintain Reusable Components
Create and reuse process steps and scripts

✅ Align with Release Cycles
Execute automation runs before and after innovation updates to catch regressions early.

✅ Leverage Cloud ALM
Integrate with Cloud ALM for full test orchestration, requirements traceability, and defect logging.

Conclusion

The SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud Test Automation Tool is not just a nice-to-have—it's your first line of defence against change-related defects in a fast-evolving cloud ERP world. Functional consultants and test engineers now have the power to automate business processes without writing a single line of code.

In future posts, I’ll deep-dive into:

  • Creating test data variants
  • Handling failed scripts

entry.title: SAP Fiori Innovation Day Bangalore -- Connection, Gratitude, AI, and Local Engineering Pride
entry.content:
The first-ever SAP Fiori Innovation Day in Bangalore was a tremendous success, bringing together 200+ participants for what became the largest SAP Fiori Innovation Day yet. This event provided local developers an opportunity to connect with customers, showcase our latest AI-powered innovations, and help us understand where to focus our development efforts.

In this post, I'll discuss the key sessions, share insights from our presentations, and highlight the remarkable contributions from our local development community.

Engineering Pride from Our Local Contributions to the UX Foundation Portfolio

We started with Ioannis Grammatikakis, SVP for UX Foundation at SAP, providing our UX strategy overview for SAP S/4HANA Cloud. His vision about intelligent UX and the humanness aspect that creates customer loyalty resonated strongly with our audience.

Ioannis Grammatikakis, SVP for UX Foundation at SAP, presenting the UX strategy and vision for SAP S/4HANA

We then demonstrated the latest AI functionality embedded across the SAP S/4HANA portfolio:

  • My Home: AI-powered app recommendations, intelligent insights cards, and Joule integration for personalized experiences
  • List Report: Natural language filtering that translates user requests into precise filter criteria
  • Object Page: AI summarization features allowing users to select sections and share results with colleagues

It was gratifying to see our local Bangalore team members demonstrating these features they directly contributed to developing. The audience engagement was particularly strong, with participants asking about integrating S/4HANA systems with Microsoft Teams services and bot platforms.

Hands-on Experience with SAP Fiori Tools

Our hands-on sessions led by Gaurav Rawat showed how SAP Fiori tools in Business Application Studio (BAS) addresses 80% of common use cases. Participants experienced the complete development cycle from template selection through deployment, with tools automatically handling metadata, annotations, and dependencies. The interactive nature of the sessions sparked excellent questions from participants, including how to configure simple filters in SAPUI5 Freestyle applications, setting up SAP Fiori tools extensions in BAS, and whether SAP Fiori apps developed through SAP Fiori tools can be containerized using Docker.

Participants working with SAP Fiori tools and exploring AI-powered development features

Interactive Demo Booths: Hands-on Exploration

Beyond the main stage presentations, participants had the opportunity to explore multiple demo booths throughout the day, each focusing on different aspects of SAP Fiori development and user experience:

Leverage SAP Fiori Apps Reference Library: Participants explored how to identify apps strategic for their business needs by leveraging the comprehensive library of available SAP Fiori applications and learning about installation options.

Intelligent Entry Experience to SAP S/4HANA: Demonstrations showcased the journey from first steps to daily excellence, featuring the latest My Home page in SAP S/4HANA systems, which serves as the standard entry page providing a personalized and unified user experience.

Seamless and Collaborative Experience with Technology-Agnostic Cards: This session focused on creating seamless user interactions through technology-agnostic cards that enhance collaboration and overall application usability.

AI-Assisted Easy Filter: A deep dive into redefining the filtering experience through innovation, featuring natural language filtering capabilities and AI-powered search functionality.

SAP Fiori Elements Fast-Track App Development: Best practices and strategies for boosting developer efficiency and building applications that can grow with business needs using SAP Fiori elements. Participants were particularly interested in the availability of public documentation for these app generation capabilities.

Interactive Demo Booths

Mindset's AI-Powered Innovation: Reimagining SAP Fiori User Experience

Our partner Mindset Consulting presented their session "Reimagining SAP Fiori: AI-Powered User Experience" showcasing their innovative document summary generator using LLM models. Their solution demonstrates how AI can automatically process and summarize documents, with the generated summaries being stored in HANA DB for efficient retrieval and reuse. This demonstration highlighted how partner solutions can extend SAP Fiori applications with advanced AI capabilities, showing practical applications of intelligent document processing in enterprise scenarios.

Developing Applications is a Team Sport, So Connections Matter

Modern enterprise applications require collaboration between business owners, user researchers, designers, enterprise architects, and developers—with AI tools increasingly becoming team members. The diverse skills of our Bangalore participants perfectly matched today's fusion development teams. The energy and engagement throughout the event will directly influence our future innovations.

SAP Fiori Innovation Day team

Gratitude for Making SAP Fiori Innovation Day a Huge Success

This event's success came from the energy and engagement of 200+ participants. Their questions, comments, and feedback provide invaluable guidance for future innovations.

Thanks to all SAP presenters for their valuable contributions. Special thanks to Mindset Consulting for sponsoring and their presentation on Reimagining SAP Fiori User Experience, and the SAP Inside Track team for integrating our UX track into their larger community event.

Watch for SAP Fiori Innovation Day in Your Region

  • Dallas, Texas, USA -- October 17, 2025 as part of the ASUG chapter meeting
  • Paris, France -- December 10, 2025

Continue Your Learning

  • Subscribe to the bi-monthly SAP Fiori Development Newsletter
  • Sign up for the SAP S/4HANA UX Roundtable in India (starting in summer 2025)
  • Join the monthly SAP Fiori Development Roundtable for U.S. and Europe if that fits your schedule better

entry.title: 在SAP BTP上创建定时运行的作业：Integration Suite（集成套件）中的实用指南
entry.content:
在企业数字化转型的过程中，自动化和定时任务的实施变得尤为关键。SAP Business Technology Platform（BTP）提供了强大的Integration Suite，使得开发者可以轻松地创建和管理集成流程，实现定时调度、数据传输等自动化目标。本文将详细介绍如何在BTP上配置一个定时运行作业，可集成例如 SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition这样的SaaS服务，更灵活地实现自定义逻辑流的定时触发与执行，并监控执行结果。

本文主要创意来源和技术支持由 Cloud ERP PSCC  @Fancy_Zou 提供。

一、进入Integration Suite界面

首先，登录到SAP BTP cockpit，导航至Integration Suite界面（如下图1）：

在左侧菜单中，点击“Design（设计）”，进入“集成和API”界面（图2），该处集中管理所有的集成包和组件。

图1 - Integration Suite 主页

 

二、选择合适的包和查看组件

在“集成和API”界面中，你可以看到你已有的所有Package（包）。选择一个需要操作的包，进入该包内可以查看和管理其包含的所有集成部件。

 

图2 - 集成和API界面

 

三、创建集成流程（Integration Flow）

在包中，点击“Add（添加）”按钮，选择Integration Flow（集成流程）作为新建部件，命名后进入其编辑界面。

在集成流程的编辑页面中，我们将根据需求配置流程步骤。在本示例中，流程较为简洁，仅保留“End（结束）”和“Receiver（接收器）”步骤。

请注意，在编辑页面中单击选中创建的步骤模块，即可以配置其详细参数。

 

图3 - 开始集成流程的编辑

 

四、添加定时触发器（Timer）

为了实现作业的定时自动触发，我们在流程中添加一个开始步骤（Start），选择Timer（计时器）类型。选中以配置该计时器参数：

  • 触发频率：例如，每5分钟触发一次
  • 开始时间（可选）：设定为立即开始或者指定时间
  • 结束时间（可选）：确定作业持续时间或终止点

这一步骤确保流程按预定时间自动启动，无需人工干预。

图4 - 添加、编辑定时器

 

五、配置业务操作（调用API）

完成计时器设置后，进入API调用步骤，我们需要特别注意准备请求体（Body）内容，否则API调用无法成功。在此步骤中，配置目标API端点、请求参数等细节。确保请求设置合理，能成功获取所需数据。

具体操作如下：

1. 添加一个需要消息返回的API调用

图5 - 添加需要消息返回的步骤

2. 准备请求体（Body）

在“调用API”之前，需要在流程中添加一个“Content Modifier（内容修改器）”步骤，用于构建请求体内容。

  • 添加一个Content Modifier步骤，例如在这里我们命名为“准备Body”。
  • 单击选中该步骤模块，可以配置其详细参数。首先在Message Header页签为接口调用创建额外的抬头参数。
  • 接着在 Message Body页签中Body区域，输入API所需的请求报文内容。
  • 你也可以使用表达式或变量动态生成请求参数，比如引用先前步骤的变量，确保请求内容符合API接口的规范。

图6 - 添加、编辑内容修改器

3. 配置API请求步骤

画一根连接线，连接“调用API” 与“Receiver”模块。选中连接线，在下方配置API请求的相关内容。

  • 选择请求方法（如POST）
  • 配置请求URL、请求头（例如Content-Type：application/json）
  • 请求体（Body）：引用之前“PrepareBody”步骤中构建的内容。

这样，API调用时就会携带完整的请求数据。

图7 - 配置API请求步骤

4. 处理API响应

  • API调用成功后，可以将响应数据绑定到变量或直接处理。你可以添加“Content Modifier”或“Receiver”步骤，用于解析返回内容（如JSON中的特定字段）或存储备用。在这里我们配置了Receiver。

5. 完成集成流配置。

对每两个集成步骤模块之间，均通过画连接线将其串接；每一个箭头指向下一个步骤模块。

图8 - 连接步骤模块

 

六、部署与监控

配置完成后，保存并部署流程。系统将按设定的时间间隔自动启动流程，调用API，实现定时数据同步或其他自动化任务。

通过左侧菜单，你可以通过BTP集成监控界面，跟踪作业的执行状态和日志信息，确保流程正常运行。

完成后可以到监控界面看到这个作业是否成功被执行了。

通过在SAP BTP集成套件中配置定时触发的流程，并提前准备好请求体内容，企业可以高效实现自动化数据操作和接口调用。掌握这些技巧，将大大提升你的流程自动化能力，为数字化转型提供有力支持。

如果你有更多需求，欢迎继续关注我们的内容或联系技术支持，共同优化你的集成方案。

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502.4: Final What's New & Product Assistance available
entry.content:
With immediate effect, the final What's New information for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502.4 is now available.

To access the final What’s New information, please use the What’s New Viewer.

In addition, the final Product Assistance (aka Application Help) for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502.4 is available. Please use the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Help Portal page to access it.

For you as a customer, we recommend to also use the Release Assessment and Scope Dependency (RASD) tool.

The Release Assessment and Scope Dependency (RASD) tool provides you with a personalized and tailored view on What’s New information based on the activated and used scope within your system. The information for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502.4 will be available in RASD within the next few days. A valid Customer S-user ID is required for login. We recommend to use RASD to prepare for upgrades and updates of your system and to focus on the new and changed features that are relevant for you as a customer.

You can use the commenting feature for What’s New. With this feature, authors can evaluate your feedback and incorporate it into the documentation, if needed. We hope that by opening this channel of communication, we can learn how to further improve our What’s New content for you. To use this feature, you need to be logged into the Help Portal. The feature will only be available in the English version of the documents. To learn more about how the feedback process works in SAP Help Portal, see Giving Documentation Feedback.

entry.title: Preliminary What’s New Information for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2508
entry.content:
Preliminary version of What’s New information and of the Product Assistance are now available for customers and partners to get a preview on the upcoming new and changed features of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2508.

You can find this preview on the current Help Portal page SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition (see What’s New section “Preview of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition2508”). To access the Preliminary What’s New information, please use the What’s New Viewer or the Release Assessment and Scope Dependency (RASD) tool.

The RASD tool provides you with a personalized and tailored view on What’s New information based on the activated and used scope within your system. The preliminary information for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2508 will be available in RASD within the next few days. We recommend using RASD to prepare for upgrades and updates of your system and to focus on the new and changed features that are relevant for you. A valid S-User ID is needed to use the RASD tool and to view all the preliminary content stated above.

For any feedback you may have, try the Commenting Feature for What's New documentation in this release. See this blog for more information.

entry.title: Rise into the Future with Agentic AI in Enterprise Asset Management
entry.content:
In 2025, "Agentic AI" has emerged as a transformative force in the world of Business. But what exactly does this buzzword mean for businesses striving to optimize their asset maintenance and operational efficiency?

The upcoming, "Rise into the Future: Expert Demo Session – SAP AI Agents in Action" hosted by SAP, offers a compelling glimpse into how agentic AI is revolutionizing asset management within SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition. You will explore the practical benefits and innovations that agentic AI brings to the table for enterprises looking to streamline their maintenance processes.

What is Agentic AI?

Agentic AI refers to intelligent AI agents that act autonomously within a business context to solve complex problems and execute tasks with minimal human intervention. Unlike traditional AI, which often requires manual inputs and oversight, agentic AI understands the broader business environment and can make decisions that align with organizational goals. This capability is especially powerful in asset management, where coordination between production schedules, resources, and maintenance plans is critical.

Transforming Enterprise Asset Maintenance

The integration of agentic AI into SAP’s cloud ERP system is designed to bring clarity, speed, and control to enterprise asset maintenance. Here’s how:

  • Alignment of Production and Maintenance: AI agents dynamically synchronize production schedules with maintenance activities, ensuring that asset upkeep does not disrupt operations. This alignment reduces downtime and maximizes asset availability.
  • Resource Optimization: By understanding resource availability and constraints, AI agents allocate maintenance tasks efficiently, preventing bottlenecks and overuse of personnel or equipment.
  • Simplified Task Management: The AI copilot feature breaks down complex maintenance tasks into clear, actionable steps. This simplification empowers maintenance teams to execute their work more effectively and with greater confidence.

To Learn More

The "Rise into the Future: Expert Demo Session" by SAP is your opportunity to see agentic AI in action. Experts will demonstrate how these AI agents work seamlessly within SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition to transform enterprise asset management. Attendees will gain insights into how AI-driven workflows can help their organizations achieve operational excellence and maintain a competitive edge.

Click here to register.

entry.title: Introducing the Success Guide: Empowering Smooth SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Implementations
entry.content:
In the world of enterprise transformation, implementing SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition is a strategic move toward agility and innovation. But with innovation comes complexity - and that’s where the Success Guide from SAP Cloud ERP Product Success / Cloud Customer Care makes a real difference.

📘What Is the Success Guide?

The Success Guide is a practical, experience-based resource shared by SAP P&E Solution Architects. It captures real lessons from global SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition projects, helping implementation teams identify potential risks early, avoid common pitfalls, and stay aligned with SAP’s Best Practices.

Unlike traditional documentation, the Success Guide is:

  • Concise and structured
  • Scenario-driven
  • Rich with practical recommendations
  • Continuously updated

It addresses critical areas like:

  • Project methodology and mindset
  • Data migration strategy
  • Configuration guidance
  • Upgrade coordination
  • Line-of-business-specific insights (Finance, Sales, Manufacturing, etc.)

🔁Updated Quarterly, Aligned with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Major Releases

The Success Guide is released in sync with major versions of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, ensuring relevance to the most recent system capabilities and restrictions (currently version 2502).

To ensure continuous accuracy and value, the content is reviewed and updated every quarter by SAP P&E Solution Architects, incorporating new insights, updated features, and evolving customer needs.

🎯Why It Matters

A successful SAP Cloud ERP project hinges on planning, execution, and learning from others’ experiences. The Success Guide directly supports this by:

✅Preventing costly missteps before go-live
✅Encouraging a true cloud mindset and Fit-to-Standard adoption
✅Promoting early, realistic data migration planning
✅Providing guidance for irreversible configurations
✅Helping align scope, organization structures, and upgrade timing

🧭 Who Should Use It?

The Success Guide is intended for:

  • SAP implementation project managers
  • Functional and technical consultants
  • Customer IT leads and internal SAP teams
  • Partners delivering SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition solutions

It’s especially relevant during the Explore, Realize, and Deploy phases of SAP Activate methodology.

🌍Where to Access It?

The latest PDF version is published in the SAP Digital Library (Preview / Download) and curated via Microsoft Lists on the SAP Cloud ERP Product Success / Cloud Customer Care SharePoint. Each version includes a changelog and new entries based on direct feedback and regional insights.

💡Final Recommendation

Success starts with preparation—make the Success Guide part of yours. Before kicking off any SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition project, make sure your team reads the Success Guide. It’s an essential companion for navigating implementation complexity with confidence. For any feedback or suggestions, feel free to contact Ecco Liu directly at ecco.liu@sap.com.

Thank you for your attention and support.

entry.title: 《Success Guide》：助力 SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 项目顺利实施的实战宝典
entry.content:
在企业数字化转型的征程中，实施 SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 是一项面向未来的、具有前瞻思维的选择，它代表着向敏捷性与创新迈进的重要一步。但创新往往伴随复杂性，这正是《Success Guide（成功指南）》能够为项目实施带来真正帮助的原因。

Success Guide 是什么？

《Success Guide》由SAP Cloud ERP产品成功团队的解决方案架构师 （P&E Solution Architects）们基于支持到的全球 SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 实施项目的真实经验编写，内容聚焦项目实践与落地，旨在帮助项目团队提前识别潜在风险、规避常见陷阱，并始终保持与 SAP 最佳实践的一致性。

它不同于传统的文档说明，更加：

  • 简明扼要、结构清晰
  • 基于场景、直击问题
  • 富含实用建议和应对措施
  • 持续更新、紧跟版本节奏

涵盖的关键主题包括但不限于：

  • 项目方法论与云思维
  • 数据迁移策略
  • 配置阶段的注意事项
  • 升级节奏与项目排期协调
  • 各业务模块的实践要点（如财务、销售、制造等）

🔁每季度更新，紧跟 SAP 主版本节奏

Success Guide 与 SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 的主版本（如当前版本 2502）保持同步更新，确保内容始终贴合最新的系统能力与限制。

为保障持续的实用性与准确性，Success Guide 的内容由 SAP Cloud ERP产品成功团队全球的解决方案架构师（P&E Solution Architects）们每季度定期审阅与更新，持续吸收项目一线反馈、新增功能特性与客户需求。

🎯为什么 Success Guide 至关重要？

一场成功的 SAP ERP 公有云项目，离不开周密的规划、有序的执行，以及借鉴他人经验的能力。《Success Guide》正是这样一个赋能工具，它帮助您：

✅在上线前预防代价高昂的问题
✅推动团队真正建立起“云思维”与 Fit-to-Standard 实施理念
✅尽早制定并测试数据迁移计划，避免临近上线时“踩雷”
✅明确哪些配置是不可逆的，慎重处理关键决策
✅帮助你合理安排范围、组织结构设计与升级时间窗口

🧭 谁应该阅读 Success Guide？

Success Guide 适用于以下群体：

  • SAP 项目实施经理
  • 业务顾问与技术顾问
  • 客户方 IT 负责人或内部 SAP 团队成员
  • 负责交付 SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 的合作伙伴

它特别适用于 SAP Activate 方法论中的 Explore（探索）、Realize（实现）、Deploy（部署）阶段。

🌍如何获取 Success Guide？

最新版 Success Guide 的 PDF 可在 SAP Digital Library (预览 / 下载) 获取，同时也由 SAP Cloud ERP产品成功团队在其Cloud Customer Care专题的SharePoint - Microsoft Lists 中进行维护和整理，每个版本都包含更新日志和新增条目，确保可追溯性。

💡最后建议

成功源于准备。《Success Guide》正是每个 SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 项目团队在启动前值得阅读的参考资料。

项目复杂性无需独自应对，愿 Success Guide 能为您保驾护航。

如有任何意见或建议，欢迎直接联系 Ecco Liu（ecco.liu@sap.com）。

感谢您的关注与支持！

entry.title: Scheduling Background Jobs in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud: A Practical Guide
entry.content:
Step 1: Create the ABAP Class with Required Interfaces

You’ll start by creating a class that contains the logic to be executed in the background.

Interfaces Implemented:

  • IF_OO_ADT_CLASSRUN: Allows testing the class directly in ADT.
  • IF_APJ_DT_EXEC_OBJECT: Defines job metadata and input parameters.
  • IF_APJ_RT_EXEC_OBJECT: Contains the actual logic to be executed in the background job.

Your class might look like this:

CLASS zcl_insert_sample_data DEFINITION
  PUBLIC
  FINAL
  CREATE PUBLIC.

  PUBLIC SECTION.
    INTERFACES: if_oo_adt_classrun,
                if_apj_dt_exec_object,
                if_apj_rt_exec_object.

  PRIVATE SECTION.
    METHODS: insert_sample_data.
ENDCLASS.
METHOD if_apj_rt_exec_object~execute.
* " This is the method that will be called by the background job
DATA: application_log           TYPE REF TO if_bali_log,
          application_log_free_text TYPE REF TO if_bali_free_text_setter.
   TRY.
   insert_sample_data( ).
application_log = cl_bali_log=>create_with_header(
                               header = cl_bali_header_setter=>create( object = 'XYZ' " Use your custom log object/subobject
                                                                       subobject = 'INSERT' ) ).
        application_log_free_text = cl_bali_free_text_setter=>create(
                                        severity = if_bali_constants=>c_severity_information " Or c_severity_success
                                        text     = 'Sample data inserted successfully.' ).

        application_log->add_item( item = application_log_free_text ).
        cl_bali_log_db=>get_instance( )->save_log(
            log                        = application_log
            assign_to_current_appl_job = abap_true  ).

      CATCH cx_root INTO DATA(lx_error).
        " Log the error message to the Application Log
*        application_log = cl_bali_log=>create_with_header(
*                               header = cl_bali_header_setter=>create( object = 'ZSAMPLE_DATA'
*                                                                       subobject = 'INSERT' ) ).
*        application_log_free_text = cl_bali_free_text_setter=>create(
*                                        severity = if_bali_constants=>c_severity_error
*                                        text     = |Error inserting data: { lx_error->get_text( ) }| ).
*
*        application_log->add_item( item = application_log_free_text ).
*        cl_bali_log_db=>get_instance( )->save_log(
*            log                        = application_log
*            assign_to_current_appl_job = abap_true  ).

        " Re-raise the exception to signal job failure to the framework
*        RAISE EXCEPTION TYPE cx_apj_rt_exec_object
*          EXPORTING
*            previous = lx_error.

    ENDTRY.

This class inserts sample data into a custom table and logs the result using CL_BALI_LOG.

📘 Step 2: Create a Job Catalog Entry in ADT

Once your class is ready, the next step is to register it in the Job Catalog using ADT.

How to do it:

  1. In ADT, go to the Project Explorer.
  2. Right-click on your package → New → Other → ABAP Repository Object.
  3. Search for Job Catalog Entry.
  4. Provide:
    • Catalog ID: e.g., ZZ1_INSER_DATA
    • Description: e.g., Insert Sample Titles
    • Execution Class: ZCL_INSERT_SAMPLE_DATA
  5. Save and activate.

 

This step makes your class available for job templates.

🧾 Step 3: Create a Job Template in ADT

Now, create a Job Template based on the catalog entry.

Steps:

  1. In ADT, right-click your package → New → Other → ABAP Repository Object.
  2. Search for Job Template.
  3. Provide:
    • Template ID: ZZ1_JOB
    • Description: Insert Sample Titles
    • Job Catalog Entry: ZZ1_INSER_DATA
  4. If your class uses parameters, map them here.
  5. Save and activate.

 

This template will be used in the Fiori app to schedule the job.

🌐 Step 4: Create an Application Job Template in Fiori

Now switch to the Fiori Launchpad.

Steps:

  1. Open the "Manage Application Job Templates" app.
  2. Click Create.
  3. Fill in:
    • Template Name: e.g., Insert Sample Titles
    • Job Template ID: ZZ1_JOB (from ADT)
    • Job Catalog ID: ZZ1_INSER_DATA
  4. Save and activate.

This step makes the job template available for end users.

Step 5: Schedule the Job in Fiori

Finally, schedule the job using the Fiori app.

Steps:

  1. Open the "Schedule Application Jobs" app.
  2. Click Create.
  3. Select your Application Job Template.
  4. Set:
    • Start Time: Immediate or scheduled
    • Recurrence: One-time or recurring
  5. Click Schedule.

🧾 Application Logging

Your class uses CL_BALI_LOG to write messages to the application log. This is essential for monitoring and debugging.

Benefits:

  • Structured logging with severity levels.
  • Integration with job monitoring tools.
  • Easy to trace job execution history.

entry.title: My AI Experience Sharing - Setting up Joule for the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition (C: Deinstall)
entry.content:
Introduction

Now Artificial Intelligence (AI) is on every professional’s mind. Everybody wants to be on the bandwagon.  I plan to write a series of blogs on this topic under a big umbrella “My AI Experience Sharing”.  This is the first mini-series: setting up Joule for the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. 

I purposely delayed my action on setting up a Joule to wait for the dust settling down during the Early Adopter phase. The intention paid off but still won’t give me a free pass 😉. There are five credible references available as listed in the Reference section, but none of them give you a good explanation on Why, especially on What in a critical step with a readable picture when you desperately need a hint.  To help all the people like me, this blog will focus on explaining those steps, what to enter, and possible mistakes to avoid. 

Note 1: The content here is drawn from my direct experience from setting up Joule for the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition (more than five times and still counting). It might be true for other SAP solutions but only use that as a reference so that I don’t feel the pressure of entire SAP world on my shoulder 😁.

Note 2: Since there are a lot of topics to discuss, I divided the mini-series into Part A Preparation,  Part B Joule Installation and Part C Deinstallation. 

Note 3: The systems I used in this mini-series are:

  • BTP Global Account: 2024-09 3SL Account
  • BTP Joule Subaccount: PSCC-AMER-3SL-PRD
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Production tenant G4X/100, http://myxxxxxxx.s4hana.cloud.sap/ 
  • Cloud Identity Services: Provisioned together with above BTP and Public Cloud tenants, xxxxxxxx.accounts.ondemand.com

Note 4: Considering some customers and consultants need to deinstall and reinstall Joule for many reasons like practicing the installation, correcting an unsuccessful installation, etc., I am adding a new blog Part C: Deinstallation here for your reference.

 

Thirteen Major Joule Deinstallation Steps

Deinstallation is relatively easy. I list these steps in the below figure so that you can cleanly remove all the components in three systems, namely the Business Technology Platform (BTP), the S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition (S/4) and the Cloud Identity Services (CIS).

Thirteen Steps to Remove Joule Installations

 

References

  1. Blog 1: Joule – End-to-End Setup Guide (for all Line of Business) Unified Approach
  2. Blog 2: Joule in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition - Setup Guide
  3. Discovery Center 1: Establish a Unified Joule Instance
  4. Discovery Center 2: Activate Joule with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
  5. SAP Help: Joule onboarding guide
  6. My Blog: My AI Experience Sharing – Setting up Joule for the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition (Part A Preparation)
  7. My Blog: My AI Experience Sharing – Setting up Joule for the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition (Part B Joule Installation)
  8. This Blog: My AI Experience Sharing – Setting up Joule for the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition (Part C Joule Deinstallation)

entry.title: 销售合同价格协议
entry.content:
业务需求

公司需求在销售合同中对特定产品品类增加10%的折扣，这样根据合同创建的销售订单都可以获得10%的折扣，尽管单独在销售订单中增加折扣是支持的，但并不能满足通过一个合同收集与客户的完整协议，并且增加了使用灵活性。

前提配置

1. 配置：定义价格维护场景

案例使用了标准配置，客户可以新建条目使用字段物料或者物料组，注意同时需要维护相应的字段表。2.配置项目类别

系统实现

条件类型DPA1, -5%折扣
